insights.description: max(ord_p(2)) > 2k for majority of k values — LDS favorable
insights.connects_to: R196-R197 LDS: k₀(p) ≥ c·q without GRH

insights.description: FCQ R(p,k) < 1 for ALL primes in 30 k values
insights.connects_to: R196-R197 FCQ: R(p,k) = q·ρ_p^{k-1} < 1 implies avoidance
R189: the 1.35x gap is real, need structural boost
Known: phantom explains why general proof needs care at k=4

trends.extrapolation: d(k) ~ exp(1.1011k) = 3.0076^k. Compare 3^k growth rate log(3)=1.0986. Deficit γ = 0.0025
Prime factor count grows ~ 0.082k + 1.1. More factors = more spectral components = stronger avoidance
Πρ ~ exp(-0.2634k). Decay rate = 0.2634/k. Need -slope > log(3)≈1.099 for N₀=0 (currently 0.2634)
Max ord grows ~ 69367566744.25k. For LDS (R197): need ord > c·k for transport argument
Budget ratio trend: +0.0030/k. IMPROVING with k. At k=100: est. ratio = 0.43
Relative gap ~ exp(-0.0079k). DANGER: gap shrinks with k

conjectures.proof_approach: FCQ (R196-R197): R = ord_p(2) · ρ_p^{k-1}. Since ρ_p < 1 (proved R191), R→0 exponentially. Need: uniform ρ_p < 1-ε for ε > 0 independent of p. This is exactly the 1.35x gap question

conjectures.statement: d(k) has ≥ 2 distinct prime factors for k ≥ 6
conjectures.proof_approach: d(k) = 2^S - 3^k. If d(k) = p^a, then 2^S ≡ 3^k mod p^a. This is a very special Diophantine condition. Zsygmondy's theorem might apply (primitive divisors of a^n - b^n)

strategies.name: LDS + FCQ Combined Attack
strategies.next_step: 1. PARTITION primes of d(k) into S_small = {p : ord_p(2) ≤ T} and S_large = {p : ord_p(2) > T}
strategies.blocker: CRITICAL: uniform ρ_p bound. Currently ρ_p < 1 proved but no uniformity

strategies.name: Operator Framework + Gap 1.35x Closure
strategies.next_step: 1. COMPUTE: budget ratio for k=3..100 — identify if it improves with k
strategies.blocker: The 1.35x gap is REAL — not an artifact. Closing it requires new math

strategies.name: DBA (Baker) + F_Z Complete Resolution
strategies.next_step: 1. IMPLEMENT Baker-Wüstholz bound computation for F_Z
strategies.blocker: Baker bounds are technically intensive (3-6 month estimate)

strategies.name: Battement Impossible (Resonance Obstruction)
strategies.next_step: 1. FORMALIZE: the triple obstruction from monotonicity
strategies.blocker: The 'triple obstruction' is heuristic, not proved

strategies.name: Hybrid Multi-scale (Pipeline-Driven Discovery)
strategies.next_step: 1. EXTEND: run Analyst for k=41..100 (compute d(k) factorizations, spectral data)
strategies.blocker: Scalability: native_decide slows exponentially with k

critical_question: Can we prove uniform ρ_p < 1-ε for ALL p|d(k)? This is the single question that, if answered, closes everything via FCQ.
recommended_action: PRIORITY: LDS + FCQ Combined Attack (score 86.4/100). NEXT STEP: 1. PARTITION primes of d(k) into S_small = {p : ord_p(2) ≤ T} and S_large = {p : ord_p(2) > T}. CRITICAL BLOCKER: CRITICAL: uniform ρ_p bound. Currently ρ_p < 1 proved but no uniformity.
lean_roadmap: PHASE 1 (IMMEDIATE): Extend native_decide avoidance to k=41..68 (SdW range)
PHASE 2 (SHORT-TERM): Formalize LDS transport lemma in Lean 4
PHASE 3 (MEDIUM-TERM): Formalize FCQ contraction for specific primes
PHASE 4 (LONG-TERM): Connect LDS+FCQ to prove uniform ρ bound
PHASE 5 (GOAL): Prove N₀(d(k))=0 for all k ≥ 3 except phantom k=4
